sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Ἀεὶ μὲν , ὦ παῖ Λαρτίου , δἐδορκά σε
sections.translation: Always , son of Laertes , have I observed you on the prowl to snatch some means of attack against your enemies . So now at the tent of Ajax by the ships where he has his post at the camp ’ s outer edge , I watch you

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: πάλαι κυνηγετοῦντα καὶ μετρούμενον
sections.translation: for a long time as you hunt and scan his newly pressed tracks , in order to see whether he is inside or away . Your course leads you well to your goal , like that of a keen-scenting Laconian hound . For the man has just now gone in ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: στάζων ἱδρῶτι καὶ χέρας ζιφοκτόνους ,
sections.translation: with sweat dripping from his head and from his hands that have killed with the sword . There is no further need for you to peer inside these doors . Rather tell me what your goal is that you have shown such eagerness for , so that you may learn from her who holds the knowledge .

sections.label: Section 14
sections.original: ὦ φθέγμ ’ Ἀθανᾶς , φιλτάτης ἐμοὶ θεῶν ,
sections.translation: Voice of Athena , dearest to me of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: ὡς εὔμαθές σου , κἂν ἄποπτος ᾖς , ὅμως
sections.translation: how clearly , though you are unseen , do I hear your call and snatch its meaning in my mind , just as I would the bronze tongue of the Tyrrhenian trumpet ! And now you have discerned correctly that I am circling my path on the track of a man who hates me , Ajax the shield-bearer .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: κεῖνον γὰρ , οὐδἐν ’ ἄλλον , ἰχνεύω πάλαι .
sections.translation: It is he and no other that I have been tracking so long . For tonight he has done us a deed beyond comprehension — if he is indeed the doer . We know nothing for certain , but drift in doubt . And so I of my of accord took up the burden of this search .

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ἐφθαρμένας γὰρ ἀρτίως εὑρίσκομεν
sections.translation:
For we recently found all the cattle , our plunder , dead — yes , slaughtered by human hand — and with them the guardians of the flocks .
Now , all men lay responsibility for this crime to him . And further , a scout who had seen him

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: πηδῶντα πεδία σὺν νεορράντῳ ξίφει ,
sections.translation: bounding alone over the plain with a newly-wet sword reported to me and declared what he saw . Then immediately I rush upon his track , and sometimes I follow his signs , but sometimes I am bewildered , and cannot read whose they are . Your arrival is timely , for truly in all matters , both those of the past

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: τά τ ’ εὶσἐπειτα , σῇ κυβερνῶμαι χερί .
sections.translation: and those of the future , it is your hand that steers me .

sections.label: Section 36
sections.original: ἐγνων , Ὀδυσσεῦ , καὶ πάλαι φύλαξ ἔβην
sections.translation: I know it , Odysseus , and some time ago I came on the path as a lookout friendly to your hunt .

sections.label: Section 38
sections.original: ἦ καὶ , φίλη δέσποινα , πρὸς καιρὸν πονῶ ;
sections.translation: And so , dear mistress , do I toil to good effect ?

sections.label: Section 39
sections.original: Ὡς ἔστιν ἀνδρὸς τοῦδε τἄργα ταῦτά σοι .
sections.translation: Know that that man is the doer of these deeds .

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: Καὶ πρὸς τί δυςλόγιστον ὧδ ’ ᾖξεν χέρα ;
sections.translation: Then to what end did he thrust his hand so senselessly ?

sections.label: Section 41
sections.original: Χόλῳ βαρυνθεὶς τῶν Ἀχιλλείων ὅπλων .
sections.translation: He was mad with anger over the arms of Achilles .

sections.label: Section 42
sections.original: Τί δῆτα ποίμναις τήνδ ’ ἐπεμπίπτει βάσιν ;
sections.translation: Why , then , his onslaught upon the flocks ?

sections.label: Section 43
sections.original: Δοκῶν ὲν ὑμῖν χεῖρα χραίνεσθαι φόνῳ .
sections.translation: It was in your blood , he thought , that he was staining his hand .

sections.label: Section 44
sections.original: ἦ καὶ τὸ βούλευμ ’ ὡς ἐπ ’ Ἀργείοις τόδ ’ ἦν ;
sections.translation: Then was this a plot aimed against the Greeks ?

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: Κὰν ὲξἐπραξεν , εἰ κατημέλησ ’ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Yes , and he would have accomplished it , too , had I not been attentive .

sections.label: Section 46
sections.original: Ποίαισι τόλμαις ταῖςδε καὶ φρενῶν θράσει ;
sections.translation: And what reckless boldness was in his mind that he dared this ?

sections.label: Section 47
sections.original: Νύκτωρ ὲφ ’ ὑμάς δόλιος ὁρμᾶται μόνος .
sections.translation: Under night ’ s cover he set out against you , by stealth and alone .

sections.label: Section 48
sections.original: Ἡ καὶ παρέστη κἀπὶ τἐρμ ’ ἀφίκετο ;
sections.translation: And did he get near us ? Did he reach his goal ?

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: Καὶ δὴ ’ πὶ δισσαῖς ἦν στρατηγίσιν πύλαις .
sections.translation: He was already at the double doors of the two generals .

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: Καὶ πῶς ἐπέσχε χεῖρα μαιμῶσαν φόνου ;
sections.translation: How , then , did he restrain his hand when it was eager for murder ?

sections.label: Section 51
sections.original: Ἐγώ σφ ’ ἀπείργω δυςφόρους ἐπ ’ ὄμμασι
sections.translation: It was I who prevented him , by casting over his eyes oppressive notions of his fatal joy , and I who turned his fury aside on the flocks of sheep and the confused droves guarded by herdsmen , the spoil which you had not yet divided .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: ἔνθ ’ εkπεσὼν ἔκειρε πολύκερων φόνον ,
sections.translation: Then he fell upon them and kept cutting out a slaughter of many horned beasts as he split their spines in a circle around him . At one time he thought that he was killing the two Atreidae , holding them in his very hand ; at another time it was this commander , and at another that one which he attacked . And I , while the man ran about in diseased frenzy ,

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ὤτρυνον , εἰςέβαλλον εἰς ἕρκη κακά .
sections.translation:
I kept urging him on , kept hurling him into the snares of doom . Soon , when he rested from this toil , he bound together the living oxen along with with all the sheep and brought them home , as though his quarry were men , not well-horned cattle . And now he abuses them , bound together , in the house .
But to you also will I show this madness openly , so that when you have seen it you may proclaim it to all the Argives . Be of good courage and stand your ground , and do not regard the man as a cause of disaster for you . I will turn away the beams of his eyes

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: αὐγὰς ἀπείρξω , σὴν πρόςοψιν εἰςιδεῖν .
sections.translation: and keep them from landing on your face .

sections.label: Section 71
sections.original: οὗτος , σὲ , τὸν τὰς αἰχμαλωτίδας χέρας
sections.translation: You there , you who bind back your captive ’ s arms , I am calling you , come here ! I am calling Ajax ! Come out in front of the house !

sections.label: Section 74
sections.original: Τί δρᾷς , Ἀθάνα ; μηδαμῶς σφ ’ ἔξω κάλει .
sections.translation: What are you doing , Athena ? Do not call him out .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: Οὐ σῖγ ’ ἀνέξει , μηδὲ δειλίαν ἀρεῖς ;
sections.translation: Hold your peace ! Do not earn a reputation for cowardice !

sections.label: Section 76
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς θεῶν · ἀλλ ’ ἔνδον ἀρκείτω μένων .
sections.translation: No , by the gods , let it content you that he stay inside .

sections.label: Section 77
sections.original: Τί μὴ γένηται ; πρόςθεν οὐκ ἀνὴρ ὅδ ’ ἦν ;
sections.translation: What is the danger ? Was he not a man before ?

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: ἐχθρός γε τῷδε τἀνδρὶ καὶ τανῦν ἔτι .
sections.translation: Yes , a man hostile to me in the past , and especially now .

sections.label: Section 79
sections.original: Οὔκουν γέλως ἥδιστος εἰς ἐχθροὺς γελᾷν ;
sections.translation: And is not the sweetest mockery the mockery of enemies ?

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν ἀρκεῖ τοῦτον ὲν δόμοις μένειν .
sections.translation: I am content that he stay within his tent .

sections.label: Section 81
sections.original: Μεμῃνότ ’ ἄνδρα περιφανῶς ὀκνεῖς ἰδεῖν ;
sections.translation: Do you fear to see a madman right before your eyes ?

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: Φρονοῦντα γάρ νιν οὐκ ἂν ἐξέστην ὄκνῳ .
sections.translation: I would not shrink from him in fear , if he were sane .

sections.label: Section 83
sections.original: Ἀλλ ’ οὐδὲ νῦν σε μὴ παρόντ ’ ἴδῃ πέλας .
sections.translation: But he will not see you now , even though you stand nearby .

sections.label: Section 84
sections.original: Πῶς , εἴπερ ὀφθαλμοῖς γε τοῖς αὐτοῖς ὁρᾷ ;
sections.translation: How could that be , if he still sees with the same eyes ?

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: ἐγὼ σκοτώσω βλέφαρα καὶ δεδορκότα .
sections.translation: I shall darken them , though their sight is keen .

sections.label: Section 86
sections.original: Γένοιτο μέντ ’ ἂν πᾶν , θεοῦ τεχνωμένου .
sections.translation: It is true : all is possible when a god contrives .

sections.label: Section 87
sections.original: Σίγα νυν ἑστὼς καὶ μέν ’ ὡς κυρεῖς ἔχων .
sections.translation: Stand silent , then , and stay where you are .

sections.label: Section 88
sections.original: Μένοιμ ’ ἄν · ἤθελον δ ’ ἂν ἐκτὸς ὢν τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: I must stay . But I would prefer to be far from here !

sections.label: Section 89
sections.original: ὦ οὗτος , Αἴας , δεύτερόν σε προςκαλῶ .
sections.translation: You there , Ajax , once again I call you ! Why do you show so little regard for your ally ?

sections.label: Section 91
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρ ’, Ἀθάνα , χαῖρε Διογενὲς τέκνον ,
sections.translation: Welcome , Athena ! Welcome , daughter sprung from Zeus ! How well have you stood by me ! I will crown you with trophies of pure gold in gratitude for this quarry !

sections.label: Section 94
sections.original: ΑΗΝΛ . Καλῶς ἔλεξας · ἀλλ ’ ἐκεῖνό μοι φράσον ,
sections.translation: A fine pledge . But tell me this — have you dyed your sword well in the Greek army ?

sections.label: Section 96
sections.original: Κόμπος πάρεστι κοὐκ ἀπαρνοῦμαι τὸ μή .
sections.translation: I can make that boast . I do not deny it .

sections.label: Section 97
sections.original: ἦ καὶ πρὸς Ἀτρείδαισιν ᾔχμασας χέρα ;
sections.translation: And have you launched your armed hand against the Atreidae ?

sections.label: Section 98
sections.original: ὥστ ’ οὔποτ ’ Αἴαντ ’ οἵδ ’ ἀτιμάσουσ ’ ἔτι .
sections.translation: Yes , so that never again will they dishonor Ajax .

sections.label: Section 99
sections.original: τεθνᾶσιν ἅνδρες , ὡς τὸ σὸν ζυνὴκ ’ ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: The men are dead , as I interpret your words .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: Θανόντες ἤδη τἄμ ’ ἀφαιρείσθων ὅπλα .
sections.translation: Dead they are . Now let them rob me of my arms !

sections.label: Section 101
sections.original: εἶεν · τί γὰρ δὴ παῖς ὁ τοῦ Λαερτίου ;
sections.translation: I see . And the son of Laertes , how does his fortune with respect to you ? Has he escaped you ?

sections.label: Section 103
sections.original: ἦ τοὐπίτριπτον κίναδος ἐξήρου μ ’, ὅπου ;
sections.translation: That blasted fox ! You ask me where he is ?

sections.label: Section 104
sections.original: Ἔγωγ ’· Ὀδυσσέα , τὸν σὸν ἐνστάτην , λέγω .
sections.translation: Yes , I do . I mean Odysseus , your adversary .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: ἥδιστος , ὦ δέσποινα , δεσμώτης ἔσω
sections.translation: My most pleasing prisoner , mistress , he sits inside . I do not wish him to die just yet .

sections.label: Section 107
sections.original: Πρὶν ἂν τί δρόσης ἢ τί κερδάνῃς πλέον ;
sections.translation: Until you do what ? Or win what greater advantage ?

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: Πρὶν ἂν δεθεὶς πρὸς κίον ’ ἑρκείου στέγης —
sections.translation: Until he be bound to a pillar beneath my roof —

sections.label: Section 109
sections.original: Τί δῆτα τὸν δύστηνον ἐργάσει κακόν ;
sections.translation: What evil , then , will you inflict on the poor man ?

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: Μάστιγι πρῶτον νῶτα φοινιχθεὶς θάνῃ .
sections.translation: — and have his back crimsoned by the lash , before he dies .

sections.label: Section 111
sections.original: Mὴ δῆτα τὸν δύστηνον ὧδέ γ ’ αἰκίσῃ .
sections.translation: Do not abuse the poor man so cruelly !
